name: Dee Dee Bridgewater
abstract: Dee Dee Bridgewater (born Denise Eileen Garrett, May 27, 1950) is an American jazz singer and actress. She is a three-time Grammy Award-winning singer-songwriter, as well as a Tony Award-winning stage actress.

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: So Mark was on it―Mark Murphy, myself, Dee Dee Bridgewater and Don Cherry. And Howard Johnson, you know. So he had all of… George Gruntz had a way with really knowing who he wanted, and um, he had all of us come over and we rehearsed this jazz opera
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Julius Handy
about_quotes.evidence: Dee Dee was my trumpet player. And, Billy was my piano player.

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Steinau
about_quotes.evidence: I remember hearing uh oh darn who was that I get I get well it seems to me that uh Billy and Dee Dee with uh hmm I can't remember who was on clarinet with that one well they they were in Amy O'Barrge's band

about_quotes.speaker: Allan Jaffe
about_quotes.evidence: Billy and Dee Dee used to play there every, Preservation Hall, and then Preservation Hall closed one night a week, and that night we used to run a concert next door for Billy and Dee Dee.

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Robinson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] You were saying something about a fellow named Dee Dee just on the tape. [INTERVIEWEE] What's his name? Dee Dee Taylor. ... He was saying drums. Bass drums.

about_quotes.speaker: Billie Pierce and De De Pierce
about_quotes.evidence: He was playing a ukulele with me at the Kingfish... Me, Dee Dee, Mealy... George, myself, Dee Dee and Mealy. That's what I thought, yeah. The River Bell, yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billie Pierce and De De Pierce Interview, 1959-10-07
